course_meta.notes: Institution inferred from file name; exact course code/title not explicitly present in the excerpt.

syllabus.title: Rectifiers, Filters and Regulators
topics: Introduction to full-wave and half-wave rectifiers
Capacitor filter
Inductor filter, LC and π-section filter
Series and shunt voltage regulator, percentage regulation
Regulator ICs 78xx and 79xx series
Introduction to SMPS
lab_assignments: Simulation of full-wave and half-wave rectifiers with and without filter using LT-SPICE to study ripple and regulation characteristics

syllabus.title: Transistor Biasing, Stability and Amplifier Analysis
topics: Introduction to transistor biasing, Q-point selection, Diode compensation
Bias stabilization and stability factors
h-parameter model of transistors
Analysis of transistor amplifiers: expressions for voltage gain, current gain, input and output impedance
Design of transistor amplifier
High-frequency model of transistor, short-circuit current gain and β cutoff frequency
lab_assignments: Simulation of transistor circuit with self bias using LT-SPICE to study Q-point parameters
Simulation of transistor amplifier with potential divider bias using LT-SPICE to study output voltage and gain for an input signal

syllabus.title: RC Coupled Amplifier; Feedback Amplifiers and Oscillators
topics: RC coupled amplifier: equivalent circuit, voltage/current gains, input/output impedances across frequency ranges
Frequency response, lower and upper cutoff frequencies, bandwidth, wideband amplifier concept
Feedback concepts: negative and positive feedback; voltage/current, series/shunt topologies
Oscillators: Barkhausen criterion, pole-zero concept
Colpitts, Hartley, Phase-shift, Wien bridge and crystal oscillators
lab_assignments: Simulation of two-stage RC coupled amplifier using LT-SPICE to study gain and bandwidth
Simulation of phase-shift oscillator using op-amp and LT-SPICE to study frequency of oscillation by varying component values

syllabus.title: Operational Amplifier and Applications
topics: Introduction to operational amplifier; ideal op-amp; differential amplifier
Constant current source (current mirror), level shifter, CMRR
Open- and closed-loop circuits; importance of positive and negative feedback
Inverting and non-inverting amplifiers; voltage follower/buffer
Applications: adder, integrator, differentiator, comparator
Instrumentation amplifier; log and anti-log amplifiers; transconductance multiplier
Precision rectifier; voltage-to-current and current-to-voltage converters; free-running oscillator
lab_assignments: Simulation of ADC using IC AD570 with LT-SPICE
Simulation of DAC using DAC0808 with LT-SPICE
Simulation of inverting and non-inverting amplifier using op-amp with LT-SPICE
Simulation of differentiator and integrator circuits using op-amp with LT-SPICE
Simulation of logarithmic and anti-logarithmic amplifier circuits using TL441 and ICL8049 with LT-SPICE

syllabus.title: Multivibrators and 555 Timer Applications
topics: Introduction to multivibrators
Monostable, bistable, and astable multivibrators using transistor and op-amp
Monostable and astable operation using 555 timer
Schmitt trigger with 555
lab_assignments: Simulation of monostable multivibrator using IC555 with LT-SPICE
Simulation of astable multivibrator using IC555 with LT-SPICE
Simulation of Schmitt trigger circuit using IC555 with LT-SPICE
